inline_comments.message:
how is this information passed? Is it in the script? Hardcoded somewhere? I can't see it in the code so i assume the script will have to pass this. 
The script developer should not have to pass this information otherwise it will be a nightmare to update the basic auth when you have 100s of CBA with the authorization headers hardcoded in the code.

Can those be derived from the current execution? Otherwise this means that every time you up the version of the CBA you have to up the version to every calls to ResourceResolution inside the code also. I think the scripts should be agnostic of the CBA name and version otherwise it will get very hard to handle changes.

I aggree it shouldn't be hardcoded in script, but I think it also shouldn't be hardcoded in library. If anybody change an API authentication credentials should also remember to update them here. This kind of data, in my opinion, should be provided as script arguments. You can store them in one place, but not in public repository (some config file, database etc.) and get them only for script call.

Is it possible to get these data from current execution? Are them stored as e.g. env variable? I aggree script should be agnostic of the CBA name and version, it should be even possible to have one script for every CBA, that's why these values should be provided, like above, as script arguments.
